message.content:
<|channel>thought<channel|>### C# API Design: Resumable Benchmark Runner

This design focuses on a sequential execution pipeline (due to the "one heavy model server at a time" constraint) with a persistent state to allow resumption after failures or timeouts.

#### 1. Core API Sketch

```csharp
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Threading;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

public record BenchmarkResult(double Score, DateTime Timestamp, string Model);

public interface IBenchmarkRepository
{
    Task<IEnumerable<BenchmarkContext>> GetPendingContextsAsync();
    Task SaveResultAsync(BenchmarkContext context, BenchmarkResult result);
    Task MarkFailedAsync(BenchmarkContext context, string reason);
}

public interface IModelRunner
{
    // Executes the heavy model within the Docker harness
    Task<double[]> RunInferenceAsync(BenchmarkContext context, CancellationToken ct);
}

public class BenchmarkEngine
{
    private readonly IBenchmarkRepository _repo;
    private readonly IModelRunner _runner;
    private readonly SemaphoreSlim _singleServerLock = new(1, 1);

    public BenchmarkEngine(IBenchmarkRepository repo, IModelRunner runner)
    {
        _repo = repo;
        _runner = runner;
    }

    public async Task ExecuteAllAsync(CancellationToken ct)
    {
        var pending = await _repo.GetPendingContextsAsync();

        foreach (var context in pending)
        {
            if (ct.IsCancellationRequested) break;

            // Ensure only one heavy model server runs at a time
            await _singleServerLock.WaitAsync(ct);
            try
            {
                await RunSingleBenchmarkAsync(context, ct);
